characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The protagonist of the story. He enters the Borderland along with Karube and Chouta when he hangs out with them one night. He is very skilled at deduction and usually is the one to figure out the design behind each of the games. Adaptational Angst Downgrade: In the manga,being forced to kill his friend Tatta in order to defeat the King of Clubs (a man Arisu came to actually like during their short time together) broke him enough that he chose to retire from playing any of the games, essentially sitting out the rest of the story's events until only the Queen of Hearts remains.In the anime,he suffers no such breakdown from the King of Clubs game and even participates in a show original game against the Queen of Spades as well as assisting in the battle against the King of Spades, something he was completely uninvolved in in the manga. Adaptational Intelligence: Manga Arisu was hardly a moron, but he was more of a practical thinker rather than an intellectual one. In the TV show, one of hisEstablishing Character Momentsshows him casually solving a5x5Rubik's cube on his phone and the very first game he and his friends play in the Borderland is very much a Diamond game rather than a Club game like it is in the manga, and he essentially solves it single handedly. Indeed, his listed specialty is changed from Heart games (games involving mental and moral stress) to Diamond games (puzzles games of logic). Age Lift: In the manga, he was a highschool student. In the TV show, he's a college drop out. Alice Allusion: 'Arisu' is the Japanese approximation of "Alice".

characters.name: Arisu Ryohei
characters.description: The protagonist of the story. He enters the Borderland along with Karube and Chouta when he hangs out with them one night. He is very skilled at deduction and usually is the one to figure out the design behind each of the games. Adaptational Angst Downgrade: In the manga,being forced to kill his friend Tatta in order to defeat the King of Clubs (a man Arisu came to actually like during their short time together) broke him enough that he chose to retire from playing any of the games, essentially sitting out the rest of the story's events until only the Queen of Hearts remains.In the anime,he suffers no such breakdown from the King of Clubs game and even participates in a show original game against the Queen of Spades as well as assisting in the battle against the King of Spades, something he was completely uninvolved in in the manga. Adaptational Intelligence: Manga Arisu was hardly a moron, but he was more of a practical thinker rather than an intellectual one. In the TV show, one of hisEstablishing Character Momentsshows him casually solving a5x5Rubik's cube on his phone and the very first game he and his friends play in the Borderland is very much a Diamond game rather than a Club game like it is in the manga, and he essentially solves it single handedly. Indeed, his listed specialty is changed from Heart games (games involving mental and moral stress) to Diamond games (puzzles games of logic). Age Lift: In the manga, he was a highschool student. In the TV show, he's a college drop out. Alice Allusion: 'Arisu' is the Japanese approximation of "Alice".

characters.name: Usagi Yuzuha
characters.description: An athletic girl who is good at feats of agility, strength, and endurance. Arisu first meets her in his second game, 5♠Tag. He briefly bonds with her as they are close to clearing the game. The next time she sees him, Arisu despairs over the loss of his friends. She takes care of him and accompanies him on his subsequent quests. Action Girl: An adept climber and survivalist, which makes her well-suited to handle the Physical games. Adaptation Dye-Job: Bright red hair in the manga and OVA, dark brown in the live-action. Alice Allusion: Her name is a reference to the White Rabbit. Deuteragonist: Usagi gets the second most focus after Arisuafter his friends are killed.

characters.name: Karube Daikichi
characters.description: One of Arisu’s best friends from the "real world". He worked as a bartender before entering the Borderland. Adaptational Angst Upgrade: The live-action series adds a personal reason for him to survive the Seven of Hearts game: he was planning to propose to the wife of his bar owner boss. Adaptational Backstory Change: In the manga, Karube managed to own his own bar despite only graduating primary school; in the live-action, he was caught making out with the wife of his bar owner boss and was fired. The Big Guy: Karube is the primary fighter of his group of friends, being the most muscular and aggressive. Dies Differently in Adaptation:In the manga, he dies offscreen after hiding from Arisu, with the sound of his bomb collar going off; in the live-action, Arisu finds him having hisOne Last Smokeand witnesses his death by bomb collar firsthand.

characters.name: Chouta Segawa
characters.description: Arisu’s other best friend who was also brought into the Borderland. Adaptational Attractiveness: He has a slightly odd design in the manga, although it isn’t uncommon for a character with his personality. In the live-action, he is played by a normal-looking and reasonably attractive actor. Adaptational Backstory Change: In the manga, Chouta is Arisu's classmate in high school; in the live-action, he's an office worker whose mom donates his paycheck to the cult they've been part of since he was a child. Adaptational Heroism: In the manga version of the Seven of Hearts game,after Arisu tries to give away his chance to survive, Chouta refuses and hides himself until the game ends; in the live-action,he restrains Shibuki to keep her from chasing Arisu. Cool Kid-and-Loser Friendship: He seems to consider himself a loser next to the well-off/Brilliant, but LazyArisu and the tough ladies man Karube. His friendsdisagree.

characters.name: Saori Shibuki
characters.description: A determined woman and the first person Arisu and his friends meet within the Borderland. She worked at a company and was recently given a promotion. She ultimately dies from losing in 7♥Hide and Seek. Adaptational Villainy: Though it's hard to call her a villain given the circumstances,she doesnotgive in as gracefully as she does in the manga during the 7 of Hearts game. Logically, she has only known the other guys for a few days at this point and has little reason to want to sacrifice her own life for them, so Chouta forces her into hiding for the rest of the game's duration after he's decided he wants Arisu to live. She does at least stop struggling and gives up with grace when she realizes there's no more time for her to find him and live, but she certainly didn't deliberately let Arisu win like she did in the manga. Dies Differently in Adaptation:In the manga, she dies offscreen after hiding from Arisu, with the sound of her bomb collar going off; in the live-action, Shibuki stops struggling in Chouta's grasp and instead hugs him in the final seconds of the game. Forgotten Fallen Friend:Arisu doesn’t even think of her in the season 3 finale when remembering all his friends who helped him survive, when even Niragi is included. Ms. Fanservice: She is an attractive buxom woman played by a gravure model andfeatured in a sex scene with plenty ofSideboob.

characters.name: No. 1 Takeru Danma "The Hatter"
characters.description: The founder of the Beach. He wanted to create a paradise in hell, though putting up with people's indulgence took a toll on him... Adaptational Heroism: In the Netflix series. Because he only has a few episodes beforehe's murdered by Aguni, his more extreme negative traits are downplayed or left out. Alice Allusion: 'Boshiya' is the Japanese word for "hat shop", in turn being a reference to the Hatter. The Caligula: After spending a long time in the Borderland, Takeru Danma slowly becomes this as the leader of the Beach. He doesn't hesitate to kill the residents who don't offer their cards to him and kills any person who betrays him. Despair Event Horizon:In the live-action series, he reaches this when Aguni begs him to put an end to the Beach and he realizes his attempt to create a paradise has failed. He’s so distraught that he points his unloaded gun at Aguni and mimics pulling the trigger,knowing Aguni will reflexively shoot him down.

characters.name: No. 2 Morizono Aguni
characters.description: The leading opposition of the Beach. His side attracts people favoring violence and fear. Adaptational Job Change: In the Netflix show, he's the No. 3 behindKuzuryū, leading toa mutinywhere the former takes power by force after Hatter dies. Anti-Hero:In Season 2, after surviving the destruction of the Beach, he firmly stands on the side of the protagonists, making himself the chief threat to the King of Spades while the rest of the face card games carry on. That said, he doesn't see himself deserving of any forgiveness for the Beach massacre and is even more of aDeath Seekerthan before, seeing the King of Spades as theWorthy Opponentworth sacrificing his life against. Awesomeness by Analysis: In episode two, he’s able to determine the tagger’s height, weight, dominant hand, and probable source of firearms training, as well as the model, ammo capacity, and firing time of their gun even before he’s taken the tagger on up close in person himself, seemingly analyzing their encounters with other players from afar. This analytical ability is likely alearned skill, as Aguni has been both a member of theYakuzaand an officer in the Japanese Self Defense Forces. Character Development:He learns to get over his guilt for Hatter’s death and not let it drive him to end his life, choosing to return to the real world and open the hat shop in Hatter’s memory.

characters.name: No. 4 Suguru Niragi
characters.description: An extremely unstable and violent member of the militants, with a rivalry with Chishiya and history that involves bullying and abuse. Adaptational Nice Guy:DownplayedIn the manga, while still the last person who leaves the site, Niragi gives a savage speech toTatta’s grave mocking his death, only weakly giving his thanks as he leaves it. In the tv show, he still expresses annoyance that he’s only alive due to Tatta’sHeroic Sacrificebut is far more brief and him thanking Tatta comes across as more genuine. Furthermore, in the manga, Niragi’s only the last person to leave because Arisu beat him unconscious while in the show, Niragi’s still conscious as Tatta dies and deliberately sticks around for a while to pay his respects.In both the manga and live-action tv series, Niragi ultimately chooses to declinepermanent residency in the Borderlands. However, in the manga, his final conversation with Chishiya shows that while he did choose to give life a 2nd chance, he seems to still be a jerk that doesn’t want to change (even if he’s not a criminal anymore unlike in the Borderlands). In the tv show, he acknowledges having been a “good for nothing jerk” and is ultimately shown to have eventually become a better person in season 3. In the manga, while still the last person who leaves the site, Niragi gives a savage speech toTatta’s grave mocking his death, only weakly giving his thanks as he leaves it. In the tv show, he still expresses annoyance that he’s only alive due to Tatta’sHeroic Sacrificebut is far more brief and him thanking Tatta comes across as more genuine. Furthermore, in the manga, Niragi’s only the last person to leave because Arisu beat him unconscious while in the show, Niragi’s still conscious as Tatta dies and deliberately sticks around for a while to pay his respects. In both the manga and live-action tv series, Niragi ultimately chooses to declinepermanent residency in the Borderlands. However, in the manga, his final conversation with Chishiya shows tha

characters.name: No. 5 Yūji Mahiru
characters.description: Adapted Out: Originally appearing as an executive who later uncovers the truth about the Dealers, he does not appear in the Netflix version.

characters.name: No. 6 Rizuna An
characters.description: Big Damn Heroes:Saves Arisu as Banda is about to suffocate him to death. Disney Death:After the fight with the King of Spades, Ann seems to die from her injuries. Presumably thanks to Kuina holding her hand and stating, “Let’s go back together”, she still ends up revived and woken up in the real world at the end. Season 3 confirms she’s the only member of the main cast who kept her memories of the Borderlands, which allows to help Arisu save Usagi and even save the latter when Banda tries to suffocate him. The Lab Rat: She was a forensic scientist in the real world and applies her knowledge to find the real murderer in the Ten of Hearts game. Sinister Shades: Subverted, she's definitely aloof, but turns out to be one of the only Execs that doesn't have some ulterior motive. The Smart Guy: Rizuna's specialty is in games of intellect and she is the mostBook Smartof the Executives, as shown in the Four of Diamonds game.

characters.name: No. 8 "Last Boss"
characters.description: Asshole Victim: As the most vile of the militants,nobody mourns him upon his death in the fire (that he started to help kill people) at the Beach. Ax-Crazy: He’s the first person to suggest killing everyone at the Beach during 10 of hearts and is very deranged overall. Bald of Evil: Is bald and arguably the most evil of the militants. Characterization Marches On: While later shown to be an unskilled swordsmanTaught by Experience, in his first appearance, Last Boss kills a tiger with a single sword strike. Admittedly Aguni kills another bypunching it, so clearlyArtistic License – Biologywas in play. Charles Atlas Superpower: He kills a tiger in aSingle-Stroke Battleduring one of the games.

characters.name: Shuntarō Chishiya
characters.description: An enigmatic man Arisu meets in the latter's second game, 5♠Tag. Resourceful and creative, but he works only for himself, so he sometimes ends up going against the greater good. Adaptational Backstory Change: His previous job as a doctor is revised in the Netflix version such that instead of starting out sociopathic, his heart grew cold after complying with his corrupted director. Abled in the Adaptation: In the manga, Chishiya is a genuine high-functioning sociopath. He simply doesn't feel any real emotions towards other people; the closest he comes isfeeling jealousy towards the King of Diamonds for having morals he chose to die for.In the Netflix show, he has just fallen into total apathy after working for a corrupt boss during his work as a doctor, similar tothe King of Diamonds'situation, but isn't truly a sociopath. Alice Allusion: 'Chishiya' is a Japanese approximation of "Cheshire", referencing the Cheshire Cat. Fittingly, he's initially introduced as a smiling figure who prefers to stay in the background at a higher vantage point than anyone else. Anti-Hero: Chishiya will never pull punches in clearing a game even it meanssacrificing other playersto reach that goal. Despite this, he ultimately finds himself become an ally and friend to the rest of the main protagonists and, apart from using Arisu as a patsy in an attempt to steal the deck of cards from Hatter, never does anything outrightantagonisticto the other players.

characters.name: Hikari Kuina
characters.description: A tall, tanned woman in dreadlocks who is usually seen alongside Chishiya. She seeks to return to her world so that she can care for her supportive mother. Abusive Parents: Her father was a brutally traditional martial arts instructor, who didn't show his child any special treatment.Furthermore, he clearly wanted a strong son to inherit his dojo, and held Hikari in low regard even before she came out as transgender. They make amends at the end of the series though. Action Girl: One of the main examples in the series.She fights Last Boss during the season one finale, wearing nothing but abikini, through a lot of dodging and her martial arts training and ends up winning. Agony of the Feet: During her fight withLast Boss, he breaks a number of glasses on the floor around her to stop her from moving. She steps on them anyway since the only other option is to let Last Boss kill her. Alice Allusion: She's a reference to the Caterpillar character: in her first few appearances she's shown with a fake cigarette (referencing the Caterpillar's hookah) and after her past is revealedshe displays a theme of growth and transformation, as she goes from a sad and abused "boy" into a strong and confident trans woman, akin to a caterpillar becoming a butterfly.

characters.name: Kodai Tatta
characters.description: Alice Allusion: Possibly to the Mock Turtle, as 'Tatta' can be read as a Japanese approximation of "turtle" and he sheds tears for all the casualties of the Ten of Diamonds game. An Arm and a Leg: Near the end of K♣Osmosis,he has his hand severed so that Arisu can use his bracelet to win against Ginji and net the final points for the team. Unfortunately, he dies from excess blood loss afterwards. Heroic Sacrifice:Mutilates his hand to remove his bracelet so Arisu can use it to score 500 points to close the gap against the King of Clubs and save their team from inevitable death, bleeding out shortly after the game ends. I Owe You My Life: Tatta helps Usagi search for a captive Arisu out of gratitude for saving him in Five of Spades. Would Hit a Girl: In the Netflix version, he pummels a female military member unconscious for shooting his friend down.

characters.name: K♠ Isao Shīrabi
characters.description: A Mercenary veteran who oversees all the Spade Games, his event is a simple manhunt: You MUST kill him to win, and he is allowed to roam ANYWHERE in the Borderlands (except other active game venues). Anti-Villain:In his final moments, he confesses to Aguni that his actions were the only way he could think of tosave the playersfrom the misery of the Borderlands. Apologetic Attacker:In his final moments, he apologizes to Aguni for all the deaths he caused, admitting that his only motivation to do so was his desperate attempt to save the players from the Borderlands in the only way he could. Death Seeker: Due to his past, where he was forced to kill one of his almost-fataly injured friends, the ENTIRE reason his game is kill-to-be-killed is this. The Dreaded: Once his presence is revealed, he becomes this to every living survivor in the Borderlands, as he hunts and mercilessly kills people with almost supernatural efficiency. It's telling that he's so feared that practically everyonesave Aguniputs off facing him until the very end.

characters.name: Team K♣
characters.description: Affably Evil: He sees Arisu as a competitor playing a game and he tries his hardest to make the game fair for both teams, while he doesn't make it easy for Arisu's team to win, he truly respects Arisu's team and them as individuals. Even Evil Has Standards: His reaction to Arisu amputating Tatta so they could win implies he wouldn't go as far as to harm severely anyone of his team or Arisu team to win. Foil: To Arisu, asThe Leaderof their respective groups ofTrue Companions, walking a path of confident reasoning in contrast to Arisu's emotional perserverence.The end of the games indicates his team chose to remain as citizens of the Borderlands while Arisu's group all chose to return to the real world. Full-Frontal Assault: He plays the entire game in the nude. Graceful Loser:When he loses his game, he takes it extremely well. He accepts his death without regrets, and spends his last few moments exchanging friendly words and advice with Arisu. His team, who clearly view each other asTrue Companionsin their last few moments, similarly accept their losses with smiles and little fear, spending their last few moments thanking him for leading them through several victorious games up until this point.

characters.name: Jack of Hearts
characters.description: Asshole Victim: The only Face Card who’s death isn’t remotely treated sympathetically. Despair Event Horizon: When he realizes that he'll be spending subsequent rounds being subjected to Banda's torture, Enji intentionally gets a game over for himself. Hiding Behind Your Bangs: Has a long streak of hair that covers his right eye, which is replaced with a device that shows which suit is on his collar. This reflects his manipulative nature. Loophole Abuse: Gets away with breaking a rule in secret because the rule states what the players must not do, and he is a citizen. More Hateable Minor Villain: Unlike the biggest antagonists such as Mira or the King of Spades, he has zero redeeming or sympathetic qualities whatsoever. Even Niragi shows more humanity than him.

characters.name: Q♠ Lisa
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: The Queen of Spades game is not shown in the manga meaning she is a character created exclusively for the adaptation. Would Hurt a Child: Of all the players, she chose a little boy as their king so that even though he cannot be tagged into the opposing side, he would be too physically weak to tag his opponents, dooming him to a Game Over. Yandere: Finds Arisu very attractive and is perfectly willing to kill Usagi to keep him for herself.

characters.name: King of Diamonds
characters.description: A conflicted, intelligent man who hosts the most difficult game of wits.He is first seen in the Beach. Named Keiichi Kuzuryū, he is an executive ranked Number 3. He sides with the Cult Faction, serving directly under the Hatter. Face Death with Dignity: Even when he is about to be dissolved by sulfuric acid, his composure never falters. Finger-Tenting: It's pretty much his default pose during season 2. The Mole: He was part of the Beach's leadership, acting as a spy for the people running the games and coordinated the Witch Hunt game. Worthy Opponent: After revealing himself as the King of Diamonds, he finds himself as this to Chishiya, both from career backgrounds that wore down their idealism after becoming disillusioned with society's hypocritical view of the value of individual human life. ThisCommonality Connectionrevelation and interaction eventually convinces Kuzuryu tosacrifice himselfto award the win to Chishiya.

characters.name: Queen of Hearts
characters.description: An elegant young woman in black who handles the most difficult game not for the faint of heart.She, named Mira Kanō, is first seen as an executive of the Beach. She is ranked Number 7 and is under the Cult Faction. Affably Evil: To Arisu and Usagi. Big Bad: The closest thing that the first two seasons have, because her games are responsible for Arisu's depressed state and she is also the last enemy to be dealt with. Fittingly, the Queen of Hearts usually overshadows her husband the King inAlice in Wonderlandstories. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: One could argue that her game is less bloody than the other games and the fact that you still win if you lose the game of croquet may make it relatively easy (at least compared to others), but make no mistake SHE wants you to feel that sense of security so she could manipulate you into giving up and she does everything she can do to make the game of croquet as painful psychologically as it can be. Cute But Psycho: A charming and attractive woman, who does everything she can to coax Arisu into quitting the game.

characters.name: Hayato Dōdō
characters.description: Adapted Out: Does not appear in Netflix edition. Alice Allusion: He's a reference to the Dodo character.

characters.name: Akane Heiya
characters.description: Alice Allusion: Her name references the March Hare. An Arm and a Leg: Her leg was badly injured during her first game, requiring amputation after it became infected. A shady doctor took care of her and provided a prosthetic, in exchange for certain... favors. Animal Motifs: Hares. She's a sexually aggressive young woman, with a prosthetic leg that resembles a rabbit's foot. Like Usagi, she's a skilled and agile player, but far more untamed and aggressive in her interactions with Arisu. Attempted Rape:Played With: In the tv show, she rather aggressively comes onto Arisu even though he clearly doesn’t want it and only backs off when Aguni arrives. However, her dialogue of thinking he was blushing and remarking he “knows [he] wants it” heavily implies that she genuinely convinced herself he wanted it (as her being sexually aggressive is a coping mechanism after her assault by the doctor) and she likely would’ve backed off when she realized he seriously didn’t want to. Gut Feeling: The ONLY reason she doesn't die right at the beginning alongside everyone else in 7♠Boiling Death? She just didn't feel like going near the instructions. It's explored of course, but she shows a VERY strong intuition for situations going forward: she avoids a vent that leads into superheated water with no way out, and even successfully avoids/detects the K♠ ambushes for a while during his event.

characters.name: Ōki Yaba
characters.description: Alice Allusion: 'Yaba Oki' can be read as a Japanese approximation for "Jabberwocky". Even Evil Has Standards: Yaba is a scumbag who manipulates the weak willed woman Shiga into being his partner by convincing her that he's the only one she can trust while secretly working with the psychopath Banda to win the game. While Shiga is hypnotized into turning on Yaba, Yaba keeps giving her the correct information she needs to live, even when the hypnotism leads her to lie to him about what he needs to live. When Banda says he would have given her the wrong info, Yaba says that he knew he didn't like him. Also Yaba disapproves of Banda's sadism, even though he makes no attempt to stop him from indulging in his proclivities.This continues in season 3, where Yaba calls out Banda’s only doing the things he does for his own pleasure and outright deems him as “scary”. This continues in season 3, where Yaba calls out Banda’s only doing the things he does for his own pleasure and outright deems him as “scary”. I Choose to Stay:Chooses to become a Borderlands citizen when given the option to go back to the real world. A Lighter Shade of Black: Yaba’s corrupt and a conman but nowhere near as vile as Banda, someone even he openly calls “scary”.

characters.name: Sunato Banda
characters.description: Adaptational Karma: In the manga, he and Yaba are last seen together in a penthouse as permanent citizens, but in the series,he’s killed in the season 3 finale. Alice Allusion: 'Banda Sunato' can be read as a Japanese approximation for "Bandersnatch", a creature mentioned inJabberwocky. Ascended Extra: In the manga, he is just a one-off character who never even interacts with the main cast. In the show, he becomes theBig Badof the TV show original third season. Asshole Victim: Is quite easily the most evil character in the series, andis killed when trying to shoot Arisu dead. Big Bad: Of season 3.

characters.name: Urumi Akamaki
characters.description: Adaptation Name Change: In the Netflix series, her last name is "Akamaki", but in the manga it was "Aramaki". Girly Girl: She dresses in a blue, frilly dress with neatly done hair. Manipulative Bitch: She forms a group with most of the players and starts using the actions of individual members to turn the others against them, picking off the players one by one. Out-Gambitted:Her scheme only keeps her alive for so long until she too gets tricked by one or more of the other players and dies. Red Herring:She is transparently manipulative and shady to the point of being too ill-concealed to be the Jack.

characters.name: Rei Morikage
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, she only appears in the live-action adaptation. Otaku: She's interested in anime and wants to work in the industry as an animator.

characters.name: Nobuaki Hida
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: The youngest member of the group, who's often looked after and helped by other members. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, he only appears in the live-action adaptation. Only Known By His Nickname: His full first name is Nobuaki, but he's only referred to as Nobu throughout the story.

characters.name: Tetsu
characters.description: Addled Addict: Has his life pretty much in shambles due to his drug addiction. The Alcoholic: While treating Shion's wounds, he drinks the alcohol instead of using it on her, claiming it helps him with his shaky hands, implying that was the standard for him. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, he only appears in the live-action adaptation. Hidden Depths: He's quite skilled at first aid thanks to injecting himself with drugs and treating himself to avoid going to the hospital and getting caught for posession of drugs. Lady in Red: What leads tohis demise. He's swayed by a vision of his future where he has sexual intercourse with an attractive woman in red dress.

characters.name: Sachiko
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, she only appears in the live-action adaptation.

characters.name: Kazuya
characters.description: Battle Strip: He takes off his jacket and rips off his shirt when he's about to fight the remaining players for the last can one more time, revealing his yakuza tattoos. The Big Guy: He has the role of the "muscle" of the group, being used by another player for tagging and blocking the escape path of Zombies to help get rid of them. This is also why he opts to be the last member of his group left to get a can in Kick the Can game, as he has the best chances of succeeding in a physical fight with other players. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, he only appears in the live-action adaptation. Heroic Sacrifice:He lets Arisu have the second to last can as his injury would make it hard for him to get it in the last round where he'd be the last member of their group left against multiple strangers. The last round indeed proves to be a hard battle for the last can, which ends in Kazuya's death. Not So Stoic: While he usually doesn't show emotion, he's visibly shaken after using a Shotgun card against a player with a Zombie card, sentencing him to death. He's also shown crying when he believes he's been doomed with a Zombie card himself.

characters.name: Natsu and Shion
characters.description: Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: Following the group's first game, Natsu is shown wearing an outfit that exposes her midriff, which is meant to further characterize her as a fashionableGirly Girl. Book Dumb: When drawing her fortune, Natsu admits that solving a riddle isn't her cup of tea. Butt-Monkey: Things rarely go well for Shion. She gets injured in her first game of Season 3, which makes it harder for her to fight, resulting in her accidentally dropping the bomb inher last game, resulting in her death in the explosion. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, they only appear in the live-action adaptation. Fan Disservice: Shion's partly exposed after Tetsu uses her shirt...to treat her wounds from getting hit with a flaming arrow.

characters.name: Masato
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, he only appears in the live-action adaptation. Hope Spot: He was close to tagging Rei. Had he managed to tag her before getting roped into playing a game withone of players bent on killing everyone with a zombie role, he likely would have survived. Nerd Glasses: He's dressed in a stereotypical nerdy fashion, including wearing glasses. We Hardly Knew Ye:He's the first one of the group to die and one of a few with no backstory even slightly hinted at.

characters.name: Ryuji Matsuyama
characters.description: Anti-Villain: He is an antagonistic force in Season 3, but it's courtesy of Banda using him for his own plans. Speaking of whom, the latter is the actualBig Badof the season. Ryuji also does end up going through aHeel–Face Revolving Door. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, he only appears in the live-action adaptation. Death Seeker: He's interested in researching the afterlife (even more so after feeling guilty foraccidentally killing his student during an experiment) and chooses to enter the Borderland in order to investigate it.For the same reason, he opts to choose "death" by letting himself sink in a water vortex in the finale. Handicapped Badass: In spite of becoming wheelchair-bound after an accident, he keeps training to remain in shape. This allows him to survive the Floating Lasers game and even jump from one train to another in Runaway Train game in spite of his handicap. Heel–Face Revolving Door: And how...He uses Usagi's grief for her father to lure her into Borderland per Banda's commands. But he also forms a genuine bond with her and the two often work together in various games. He's then tasked by Banda with shooting Usagi in the promise of being shown the true world of death, which he seriously considers, but gives up on, even using his wheelchair to block a door from closing, thus saving Arisu and Usagi from being stuck in a room in Possible Futures game and preventing them from losing another point each (along with messing the entire plan for everyone). He then once again attempts to shoot Usagi after clearing the games, but ultimately gives up on it... only to try to drag her to the void of death with him before changing his mind on that as well and letting her go.

characters.name: Yuna and Sota Itsuki
characters.description: Big Brother Instinct: Sota is incredibly protective of Yuna throughout the games.The Possible Futures game leads to this trope causing his death, as Sota is persuaded to follow the doors which show Yuna's happy marriage and motherhood, in turn leading him to a penalty door where he loses enough points. Brother–Sister Team: They stay together during the games for as long as they can, at least until Possible Futures game eventually forces them to separate. Canon Foreigner: Much like every character debuting in Season 3, they only appear in the live-action adaptation. Go Out with a Smile:Sota dies as happily as he can given the circumstances, with the future shown depicted in his final room being Yuna's wedding.

characters.name: Himari
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: Just like every character debuting in Season 3, she doesn't appear in the original manga series. Disney Villain Death:She falls to her death in the penultimate Joker game titled Tokyo Bingo Tower. Nice Girl: She is a joyful and kind person that never lashes out at anyone, especially Usagi. We Hardly Knew Ye:She was the second person in Usagi's team that died, yet we never got any hints of her backstory.

characters.name: Taro
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: He doesn't make a single appearance in original manga series. Disney Villain Death:He falls off the Tokyo Bingo Tower and dies. We Hardly Knew Ye:Even though he outlived Masato, Natsu and Shion, his backstory was never detailed. Yank the Dog's Chain: After he pressed on the number 68 buzzer, leaving him and the other players with only one more buzzer to clear the game,he gets knocked off the tower by the balls and dies. Totally not the best way to go, right?

characters.name: Juri
characters.description: Ain't Too Proud to Beg:When she ran out of time to jump to another train in the Runaway Train game, she begged the players that managed to do so to save her. Canon Foreigner: Doesn't appear in the manga series. Foregone Conclusion:Given how she wasted all of her gas masks and remained in the train where she has to survive two more train cars with poison gas, her fate is pretty much sealed. Hate Sink: Downplayed. Even though she is nowhere near as bad as militants of the beach or Ikeno, she is still nothing but an ungrateful and unpleasant person, blaming and lashing out at both Taro and Usagi for 'dooming' everyone in the Runaway Train game and putting the blames on the latter for the deaths of the duo that tried to jump to another train. Jerkass: She lashes out at Usagi for "dooming" all of the players in the Runaway Train game by having them waste their masks on the train car that turned out to be not poisonous, leaving them unable to clear the game, calling her a "daughter of fraud" and a "liar", when there was no way she could have known whether it was poisonous or not. Juri also blames her for the deaths of two people that got hit by an approaching pillar after she encouraged every remaining player to jump to the other train.

characters.name: Ikeno
characters.description: Asshole Victim: He is a sociopathic prick that opts to kill zombie players by placing a shotgun card rather than turn them back to humans to the point where he orders his fellow human players to kill anyone that converts into zombies regardless of their reluctance.You're not gonna feel bad for him when he gets struck down by a laser for failing to outnumber zombie players. Canon Foreigner: Doesn't appear in the manga series. Dies Wide Open:His eyes were left open when he died. Faux Affably Evil: Applauds and praises Nobu for killing a player that zombified Masato. It however didn't stop Ikeno from trying to have him killed when he found out he got infected. Hero Killer: After Nobu killed a zombie player to prevent Ikeno and his team from killing Masato,they still did it anyways.

characters.name: Mano
characters.description: All There in the Script: Even though no one has ever said Mano's name in the series, the Japanese subtitles however reveal his name. Asshole Victim: He's clearly not any better than his boss Ikeno, given how the former was the one to suggest him to reduce the number of zombie players by killing them. No one shed a tear when Manogets struck down by a laser for failing to outnumber zombies.Not even Ikeno. Canon Foreigner: Doesn't appear in the original manga series. The Dragon: As stated above. Evil Genius: You can't really deny his strategies are pretty clever and smart.
